title: aws-ssm: ?field= silently ignored in get(), full raw value returned
description: Trigger: construct AwsSsmBackend from_uri('secretx://aws-ssm/my-param?field=password') where the SSM parameter contains a JSON string like {"password":"s3cret"}. Bad behavior: get() returns the entire raw JSON string, not the extracted password field. The ?field= query param is silently ignored because AwsSsmBackend has no 'field' member and from_uri never checks for it. Users familiar with aws-sm (which does support ?field=) will assume SSM does too and get wrong values with no error. Same silent-ignore affects doppler, bitwarden, and keyring get() calls. Fix: validate at construction time that ?field= is absent, and return SecretError::InvalidUri('aws-ssm does not support field extraction; remove ?field=') if it is set.
